  "findings": [
    "Worn sheave grooves causing the belt to ride too low in the sheave (bottoming out)"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace the worn sheave with a new one matching manufacturer specifications before final commissioning",
    "Inspect the belt for premature wear or damage and replace if necessary"
  ],
  "rationale": "The sheave grooves are worn to the point where the belt is riding low (bottoming out in the groove). According to manufacturer guides, belts must drive on the sidewalls of the sheave; bottoming out causes slippage, rapid belt wear, and loss of power transmission, making the installation defective.",
  "references": [
    "Manufacturer installation and maintenance manuals for V-belt drives"
  ]
}
